methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Brimstone, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Cody Finke and Hugo Leandri, has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector comparables (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), supplemented by reference to a broader universe of 8 private-sector peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, anchoring to the $100M+ raised as a proxy for current value, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying a $200M valuation. The peer-median method uses the three same-stage, same-sector comparables, which exhibit a range of $110M–$200M with a median of $150M. These two approaches yield an arithmetic triangulation: low of $128M, median of $175M, and high of $230M.

The $175M median estimate represents a reasonable midpoint for a Series A company in climate and energy with demonstrated capital attraction. Key uncertainties center on revenue scale relative to peers (not provided), the applicability of the 2.0x stage multiplier to Brimstone's specific risk and growth profile, and valuation volatility across the private climate tech market. A working range of $150M–$200M appears most defensible given the peer median and the upper bound of stage progression.
